title: Quincy approves money for new firefighting gear after PFAS found in old gear
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy approved $2.65 million to replace firefighter gear due to PFAS chemicals linked to cancer and health risks.
Firefighters will receive two sets of PFAS-free gear each, after concerns over old equipment's safety raised by testing.
Massachusetts will ban sales of gear with intentionally added PFAS starting January, following August 2024 legislation.

title: With his new doc  ‘Henry David Thoreau,’ Don Henley returns to Walden
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Don Henley, Eagles co-founder, executive produces 'Henry David Thoreau' documentary airing March 30-31 on PBS.
Henley founded The Walden Woods Project in 1990 to preserve land near Thoreau's historic site from commercial development.
The documentary features narration by George Clooney and voices from actors like Jeff Goldblum and Meryl Streep.

title: World Cup friendly match at Gillette was an ‘amazing’ off-day field trip for the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Celtics attended a France vs. Brazil soccer match at Gillette Stadium for team bonding.
France beat Brazil 2-1 with a goal from Kylian Mbappe; Celtics' Hugo Gonzalez exchanged jerseys with him.
Coach Joe Mazzulla noted similarities between basketball and soccer, highlighting lessons from the match.

title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard led the Celtics with 36 points in a 109-102 win over the Hawks.
Jayson Tatum contributed 25 points, 12 rebounds, and 5 assists despite shooting challenges.
Celtics' Jaylen Brown is day-to-day with Achilles tendinitis, missing the game against the Hawks.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned this season following a ruptured Achilles, motivated by the Boston Celtics' championship prospects.
The Celtics are in second place, boosting Tatum's decision to rejoin the team for the regular season's final stretch.
Tatum, recovering from surgery, shows strong rebounding and versatile play but struggles with shooting percentages.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was killed in a shooting at a Lowell apartment complex on Pawtucket Boulevard.
The incident occurred after an altercation between two armed men who knew each other, officials said.
The Middlesex DA's Office and local police are investigating; no ongoing threat to the public reported.

title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connecticut Sun sold to Houston Rockets owners, the Fertitta family, for $300 million, set to relocate by 2027.
Team will be renamed the Houston Comets, reviving the name of Houston's former WNBA team disbanded in 2008.
Steve Pagliuca's attempt to move the Sun to Boston last year was blocked by the WNBA, ending his $325 million deal.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Daredevil: Born Again' Season 2 featuring Charlie Cox is now available on Disney+.
'From the World of John Wick: Ballerina' starring Ana de Armas is streaming on HBO Max.
'Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice', a Vince Vaughn action-comedy, is available on Hulu.

title: Celtics’ Jaylen Brown (left Achilles tendinitis) out vs. Hawks
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown will miss the Celtics' game against the Hawks due to left Achilles tendinitis.
Brown was initially listed as questionable for the game, but his status is now 'day-to-day' according to coach Joe Mazzulla.
The Celtics hold a 48-24 record and are second in the Eastern Conference, largely due to Brown's strong performance this season.

title: ‘Where does it end?’: Readers react to gas-powered leaf blower bans
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Arlington and Lexington banned gas-powered leaf blowers, citing noise and emissions concerns.
73% of over 250 Boston.com readers oppose the ban, citing practicality and impact on businesses.
Supporters of the ban highlight noise pollution and health issues, advocating for quieter, battery-powered options.
